data.description: VINCI, a French public limited company, has a share capital of €1,455,643,262.50. The company is headquartered at 1973, boulevard de la Défense in Nanterre, France. data.description: A recent report revealed that over 2,000 students faced expulsion due to violating academic integrity policies at a prominent university last year. The university's authorities emphasized the importance of upholding academic honesty and stated that disciplinary actions were necessary to maintain the institution's reputation. Additionally, it was highlighted that the number of students penalized for academic dishonesty had significantly increased compared to the previous year, showcasing a concerning trend. The university plans to implement stricter measures and educational campaigns to deter future violations and promote ethical behavior among its student body.

data.title: Consumer Cellular names Zach Johnson as brand ambassador, expanding golf roster for Americans 50+. Johnson, two-time Major champion, to represent the company as he competes in PGA TOUR and PGA TOUR Champions. (Word count: 25)
data.description: Consumer Cellular, the wireless provider catering to Americans aged 50 and above, has recently added two-time Major champion Zach Johnson to its team of brand ambassadors. The company, based in Scottsdale, Arizona, made this announcement on March 4, 2026. Johnson will be representing Consumer Cellular as he competes on both the PGA TOUR and PGA TOUR Champions. This move is part of the company's efforts to expand its presence in the golfing community and connect with consumers in that demographic.

data.title: Solo exhibition by Rania Matar, "Where Do I Go? لوين روح," at Leica Gallery Boston from 27th March to 31st May 2026.
data.description: Rania Matar's solo exhibition, titled Where Do I Go? لوين روح, will be showcased at Leica Gallery Boston from March 27, 2026, to May 31, 2026. The exhibition's official opening reception will take place on March 4, 2026, in TEANECK, N.J. Leica Camera Inc. invites attendees to view Matar's work during this two-month exhibition period.

data.title: Court of Appeals in Washington allows investigation into Archdiocese of Seattle's handling of child sex abuse claims, advancing transparency for survivors. - March 4, 2026 #PCVA #Seattle #CourtOfAppeals
data.description: A recent ruling paves the way for complete transparency regarding the Church's management of abuse claims. This landmark decision is a major step forward in offering transparency to survivors of clergy abuse. The law firm Pfau Cochran Vertetis Amala PLLC (PCVA Law) has hailed this ruling as a significant victory. The decision, made in Seattle on March 4, 2026, is expected to bring long-awaited transparency into the handling of these critical issues. This ruling is set to reveal important information to provide insight into a crucial matter impacting many individuals.

data.title: Tim Greiner of Pure Strategies named to the MO 100 Top Impact CEO list for his leadership and vision.
data.description: Tim Greiner, the Managing Director of Pure Strategies based in Gloucester, Massachusetts, has been recognized for his exceptional leadership, vision, and influence in driving transformative change. He has been selected as one of the top CEOs on Big Path Capital's prestigious MO 100 Top Impact CEO list for 2026. This accolade underscores Greiner's significant contribution to creating a positive impact, highlighting his exemplary performance in the industry.

data.title: Bath & Body Works stock rises despite sales decline. Q4 EPS at $2.05. Sales of $2.724B, down 2%. U.S.-Canada stores decline 2.6%. International sales increase 8.6%. Management focuses on innovation and Amazon push.
data.description: Shares of Bath & Body Works, Inc. (NYSE: BBWI) surged following a strong profit beat despite a decrease in sales and continued softness in the U.S.-Canada business segment. The company is focusing on a "Consumer First" reset strategy, emphasizing faster innovation, wider distribution, and expansion into Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN). However, the outlook remains cautious. In the fourth quarter, the company reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.05. Quarterly sales amounted to $2.724 billion, a 2% decline from the previous year but surpassing analysts' expectations of $1.336 billion. Segment performance breakdown includes a 2.6% drop in sales for Stores – U.S. & Canada reaching $2.05 billion, a 2.5% decrease in Direct – U.S. & Canada revenue totaling $579 million, and an 8.6% rise in International sales to $91 million. The quarterly gross profit and other detailed financial information can be found on Benzinga.com.
